name: Andouille-Tasso Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake cornbread according to package directions (use an unsweetened mix) and let cool to room temperature.
2. In a 2-1/2-quart saucepan, bring chicken stock to a boil. Add the finely chopped Andouille and Tasso, reduce heat, and simmer for about 1 hour until meats are soft and tender.
3. In a 12-inch skillet, melt margarine over medium heat. Quickly saute onions, green onions, celery, mushrooms, bell pepper, parsley, and garlic until wilted (do not overcook).
4. Crumble the cooled cornbread in a mixing bowl.
5. Stir in the sauteed vegetable mixture and all of the meats, blending thoroughly.
6. Gradually add the chicken stock you made, moistening the dressing. Taste before adding salt and pepper; there may already be enough seasoning from the Tasso and Andouille.
7. Use with Stuffed Veal Pocket.
NOTE: The best way to enhance a traditional New Orleans veal pocket is to stuff it with this cornbread dressing spiced with Andouille and Tasso.
